title: Lustre I/O optimization documentation retrieval
difficulty_justification: Requires reading and synthesizing multiple I/O optimization recommendations from docs to form an actionable guidance summary.
query_text: What are the documented best practices for optimizing I/O performance for large parallel jobs on the Lustre scratch filesystem? I want to reduce contention.

eval_criteria.gold_answer: From the storage user guide, the documented I/O best practices for large parallel jobs are: 1. Use Lustre striping: set stripe count to the number of OSTs you plan to use (`lfs setstripe -c 8 /lustre/scratch/mydir`). 2. Avoid small files: consolidate into HDF5 or tar archives to reduce metadata overhead. 3. Collective I/O: use MPI-IO collective operations (e.g. MPI_File_write_all) for parallel writes. 4. Stage data locally: copy input data to $TMPDIR (local NVMe) at job start for I/O-intensive jobs.

checkpoints.binary_pass_condition: final_answer mentions lfs setstripe or Lustre striping
final_answer mentions collective I/O or MPI_File_write_all
